masthead.title_html: Reason Your Way Through the World Cup
masthead.title_plain: Reason Your Way Through the World Cup
masthead.dek: Twenty reasoning-driven questions on the men's FIFA World Cup.
masthead.footer_html: Figures verified against FIFA and reference sources.

dimensions: first host outside Europe and South America
decade of the single-tournament goal record
most goals by one side in a match
first host eliminated in group stage
nation that kept the original trophy
deepest African run in the tournament
nation present at every World Cup

questions.cat: Winners · Title count by a non-Brazil power
questions.q: Set Brazil aside. Which country has won the most men's World Cups, lifting the trophy four times across two different eras?
questions.detail: Germany has won four titles (1954, 1974 and 1990 as West Germany, then 2014) — most of any nation other than Brazil's five.
questions.why: Brazil leads with five, so the real puzzle is second place — and Germany ties Italy on four but edges ahead as the team people reach for less quickly.
questions.more: Italy also has four (1934, 1938, 1982, 2006); the two are level, so naming either is defensible — through 2022.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Which team has won the most World Cup titles' (Brazil 5; Germany 4; Italy 4)

questions.cat: Hosts · Geography of the host rotation
questions.dimension: first host outside Europe and South America
questions.q: For its first 40 years the World Cup stayed in Europe and South America. Which country finally broke that pattern as the first host on neither continent?
questions.detail: Mexico hosted the 1970 World Cup — the first held outside Europe and South America, and the first in North America.
questions.why: The reflex answer is the United States, but the USA didn't host until 24 years later; Mexico got there first.
questions.more: Mexico's win let it later become the first country picked to host three times (1970, 1986, and 2026) — through 2022.
questions.source: Wikipedia '1970 FIFA World Cup'; ESPN, 'How the 1970 World Cup in Mexico changed global soccer'

questions.cat: Scoring · Era of a single-tournament feat
questions.dimension: decade of the single-tournament goal record
questions.q: One player scored 13 goals at a single World Cup — a record untouched ever since. In which decade did he do it?
questions.detail: France's Just Fontaine scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup in Sweden, still the most in one edition.
questions.why: Routing to the decade defeats anyone who only half-remembers the name — you have to place the era of a record nobody has approached in 16 tournaments since.
questions.more: His 13 more than doubled the next-best tally that year (six, by Pelé and Helmut Rahn) — through 2022.
questions.source: Olympics.com, 'Most goals in a single FIFA World Cup'; FIFA, 'Just Fontaine 1958'
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (decade, not year) — difficulty hard

questions.cat: Scoring · Goals by one team in a single match
questions.dimension: most goals by one side in a match
questions.q: What is the most goals one team has ever scored in a single World Cup match — a tally hit only once, in a 1982 rout of El Salvador?
questions.detail: Hungary beat El Salvador 10–1 in 1982, the only time a team has reached double digits in a World Cup match.
questions.why: Most people guess seven or eight; the true ceiling sits surprisingly high at exactly ten, and it has stood for over 40 years.
questions.more: Substitute László Kiss scored a hat-trick in that game in about seven minutes — the only sub ever to do so — through 2022.
questions.source: Wikipedia, 'Hungary v El Salvador (1982 FIFA World Cup)'; Opta Analyst, 'Biggest World Cup Wins'

questions.cat: Scoring · All-time leading scorer's count
questions.q: How many career World Cup goals did the all-time men's record holder finish with — a German striker who set the mark at the 2014 tournament?
questions.detail: Miroslav Klose scored 16 World Cup goals across four tournaments (2002–2014), passing Ronaldo's 15 in the 2014 semifinal.
questions.why: Spread over four World Cups, the record is lower than the gaudy single-tournament feats suggest — 16 is a guess-low trap.
questions.more: He broke the record in Germany's 7–1 win over hosts Brazil, then won the title days later — through 2022.
questions.source: FIFA, 'Miroslav Klose's goalscoring record' (16 goals); BBC Sport
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (count, not name) — difficulty med

questions.cat: Format · Era of a field expansion
questions.q: The World Cup used a 32-team field for many years before 2026's jump to 48. In which decade did the tournament first expand to 32 teams?
questions.detail: The field grew from 24 to 32 teams at the 1998 World Cup in France, producing 64 matches.
questions.why: Routing to the decade stops a giveaway year; the 32-team era feels modern, but it actually began back in the 1990s.
questions.more: That format lasted through 2022; 2026 expands to 48 teams and 104 matches — the largest ever.
questions.source: Sofascore, 'Evolution of FIFA World Cup format'; BBC Sport, 'FIFA to expand to 48 teams'
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (decade, not year) — difficulty med

questions.cat: Upsets · Host nation collapse
questions.dimension: first host eliminated in group stage
questions.q: Hosts almost always survive the group stage. Which country became the first World Cup host ever knocked out in the first round?
questions.detail: South Africa, hosting in 2010, became the first host nation eliminated in the group stage, edged out on goal difference behind Mexico.
questions.why: For 79 years no host had failed this early; the answer is the first to break that streak, not a more recent example.
questions.more: Qatar later joined them in 2022, but South Africa was first — through 2022.
questions.source: Wikipedia '2010 FIFA World Cup Group A'; SoccerAmerica, 'South Africa exit marks new low for host'

questions.cat: Trophies · The retired Jules Rimet Trophy
questions.dimension: nation that kept the original trophy
questions.q: The original World Cup trophy was awarded permanently to the first nation to win three titles. Which country got to keep it for good?
questions.detail: Brazil's third title (1970) let it keep the Jules Rimet Trophy permanently, per a rule set in 1930.
questions.why: Per Jules Rimet's stipulation, a third win meant permanent ownership — Brazil reached that mark first.
questions.more: FIFA then commissioned today's trophy, debuting in 1974; the Rimet cup was stolen in 1983 and never recovered — through 2022.
questions.source: FIFA, 'The story of the FIFA World Cup trophy'; Britannica, 'Jules Rimet Trophy'
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (rule, not year) — difficulty med

questions.cat: Finals · Most finals reached without a title
questions.q: Which country has reached the most men's World Cup finals while never winning the tournament — losing the final three separate times?
questions.detail: The Netherlands lost three World Cup finals (1974, 1978, 2010) and is the only team to finish every final it reached as runner-up.
questions.why: Germany and Argentina also lost three or four finals, but they won titles too — the slicer keeps only nations that never lifted the cup.
questions.more: Their losses came against West Germany, Argentina, and Spain — through 2022.
questions.source: beIN Sports, 'The Netherlands and Its Three Lost World Cup Finals'; Wikipedia 'Netherlands at the FIFA World Cup'

questions.cat: Upsets · Decade of the Maracana shock
questions.q: Hosts Brazil needed only a draw in front of nearly 200,000 fans but lost the deciding match to Uruguay. In which decade did this 'Maracanazo' happen?
questions.detail: Uruguay beat Brazil 2–1 at the Maracanã on 16 July 1950 to win the title in the final-round decider.
questions.why: Routing to the decade forces you to place one of football's most famous upsets in time rather than just name the teams.
questions.more: Brazil had thrashed Sweden 7–1 and Spain 6–1 in the final round before the collapse — through 2022.
questions.source: FIFA, 'Uruguay's stunning upset of Brazil'; Wikipedia 'Uruguay v Brazil (1950 FIFA World Cup)'
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (decade, not year) — difficulty hard

questions.cat: Geography · Best finish by an African nation
questions.dimension: deepest African run in the tournament
questions.q: No African team had ever reached a World Cup semifinal until one did in 2022, finishing fourth. Which country was it?
questions.detail: Morocco reached the 2022 semifinals and finished fourth — the best result ever by an African nation.
questions.why: Reflex names Cameroon or Ghana for African milestones, but neither got past the quarterfinals; Morocco broke the ceiling.
questions.more: Morocco beat Spain and Portugal before losing to France in the semifinal — through 2022.
questions.source: CNN, 'Morocco first African team to reach the World Cup semifinals'; Time

questions.cat: Records · Only nation in every tournament
questions.dimension: nation present at every World Cup
questions.q: Which is the only country to have appeared at every single men's World Cup, missing none since 1930?
questions.detail: Brazil is the only nation to play in all 22 World Cups through 2022; Germany has 20, Italy and Argentina 18.
questions.why: Powerhouses like Italy and Argentina have all missed at least one edition — only Brazil's streak is perfect.
questions.more: Italy, despite four titles, failed to qualify for both 2018 and 2022 — through 2022.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Which national team has participated in every FIFA World Cup'; Wikipedia 'FIFA World Cup records'

questions.cat: Winners · Era of a record fourth-decade title
questions.q: Germany won World Cups in four different decades. In which decade did it claim its most recent title, beating Argentina in the final?
questions.detail: Germany won the 2014 World Cup in Brazil, beating Argentina 1–0 in extra time — its first as a reunified nation.
questions.why: Routing to the decade rewards knowing Germany's title spread without handing over the year.
questions.more: It was Germany's fourth star, after 1954, 1974, and 1990 — through 2022.
questions.source: Britannica, 'Miroslav Klose'; NESN, 'Most World Cup wins by country'
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (decade, not year) — difficulty med

questions.cat: Hosts · Number of host cities in 2026
questions.q: The 2026 World Cup is spread across three countries. How many host cities are staging matches — a guessable round number?
questions.detail: The 2026 World Cup uses 16 host cities: 11 in the United States, three in Mexico, and two in Canada.
questions.why: With 48 teams it's tempting to guess far more venues, but the tournament concentrates into exactly 16 cities.
questions.more: It's the first World Cup hosted by three nations, with 104 matches in all.
questions.source: U.S. Soccer, 'FIFA Announces 16 Cities to Host 2026'; FIFA host cities page

questions.cat: Scoring · Goals by one team in a single edition
questions.q: Which country scored a record 27 goals in a single World Cup, yet still lost the final that year?
questions.detail: Hungary's 'Magical Magyars' scored 27 goals at the 1954 World Cup, a single-edition record, but lost the final 3–2 to West Germany.
questions.why: The twist is that the highest-scoring team ever didn't win — so the reflex 'they must be champions' fails.
questions.more: They beat South Korea 9–0 and West Germany 8–3 in the group stage before the 'Miracle of Bern' final — through 2022.
questions.source: FIFA, 'Hungary's incredible goal haul'; Wikipedia '1954 FIFA World Cup'

questions.cat: Finals · The only goalless final
questions.q: Only one men's World Cup final has ever finished scoreless after extra time, forcing a shootout. Which decade saw that final?
questions.detail: The 1994 final, Brazil vs. Italy, ended 0–0 after 120 minutes — the only goalless final — with Brazil winning on penalties.
questions.why: Routing to the decade tests whether you can place the first-ever shootout final without being handed the year.
questions.more: Roberto Baggio skied the decisive penalty; it was the first World Cup final decided by a shootout — through 2022.
questions.source: Guinness World Records, 'First penalty shoot-out in a FIFA World Cup final'; Wikipedia '1994 FIFA World Cup final'
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (decade, not year) — difficulty hard

questions.cat: Winners · First champion in tournament history
questions.q: Which nation won the very first World Cup, taking the title as host of the inaugural tournament?
questions.detail: Uruguay hosted and won the first World Cup in 1930, beating Argentina 4–2 in the Montevideo final.
questions.why: People often reach for an old European power, but the first champion was the South American host, Uruguay.
questions.more: Thirteen invited teams played; there were no qualifiers — through 2022.
questions.source: Olympics.com, 'First FIFA World Cup 1930 Uruguay'; FIFA world-cup champions article

questions.cat: Records · Fastest goal ever scored
questions.q: The fastest goal in men's World Cup history was scored in just 11 seconds in 2002. Which country's striker scored it?
questions.detail: Turkey's Hakan Şükür scored after 11 seconds against South Korea in the 2002 third-place match — the fastest World Cup goal ever.
questions.why: The setting is the easily-overlooked third-place game, and the scorer's nation — Turkey — isn't a usual suspect.
questions.more: It beat Vaclav Masek's 15-second strike from 1962; Turkey won the match 3–2 — through 2022.
questions.source: FIFA, 'Hakan Şükür fastest goal 2002'; Guinness World Records
questions._mechanism: non-decorative route (country, not player) — difficulty hard

questions.cat: Winners · First European win off its home continent
questions.q: For decades, every World Cup held outside Europe was won by a South American team. Which European nation finally broke that, winning in South Africa?
questions.detail: Spain won the 2010 World Cup in South Africa, the first European side to win a tournament held outside Europe.
questions.why: The slicer removes every European title won at home, leaving the first one earned on another continent — Spain.
questions.more: Spain beat the Netherlands 1–0 on an Andrés Iniesta goal in extra time — through 2022.
questions.source: NPR, 'Spain wins 2010 World Cup'; ESPN, 'Spain at the men's World Cup'
questions._mechanism: slicer (removes home-soil European titles) — difficulty hard

questions.cat: Women's game · Most titles by any nation
questions.q: In the women's game, one nation towers over the rest with four World Cup titles. Which country is it?
questions.detail: The United States women have won four Women's World Cups (1991, 1999, 2015, 2019), more than any other nation.
questions.why: Germany is the only other multiple winner with two, so the U.S. total of four is in a class of its own.
questions.more: No country besides the U.S. and Germany has won more than once — through the 2019 edition.
questions.source: Wikipedia 'United States at the FIFA Women's World Cup'; NBC Sports past-winners list
